The Story
Available for Freelance

Your vision.
My craft.

I am Ahmed Mobasher, a Computer Science and Engineering student with a mind that loves code and an eye that refuses to settle for anything ordinary. My story did not start in a classroom. It started in 2016, when I fell in love with making videos.

Back then, there was no plan. No business. No ambition to monetize anything. There was just a camera, a timeline, and a genuine obsession with telling stories through footage. I filmed and edited videos for my brother's and friends' YouTube channels, pouring real care into every cut, every transition, every moment I could make feel alive. I was not getting paid. I did not care. The work itself was the reward.

I kept going like that for years, quietly building a craft I did not yet have a name for. Then in 2019, something shifted. I discovered freelancing, and for the first time I realized that the skill I had been giving away for free was something the world was willing to pay for. That realization did not make me greedy. It made me serious.

In 2020, my journey as a freelancer officially began. What started with video editing slowly grew into graphics design, where every pixel became a decision and every layout a statement. I learned to think in color, type, and composition. And as the years passed, that visual instinct found its way into web design and app development, building digital experiences that do not just function but actually feel like something.

And when I step away from screens entirely, I am deep into robotics, because sometimes you need to build the future with your hands first before you code it into existence.

The Bigger Story

But there is another chapter to this story, one that began not with inspiration, but with frustration. When I first decided to launch my own business, I ran headfirst into a wall that nobody had warned me about. Not the lack of talent. Not the lack of ideas. The lack of infrastructure. The right tools were scattered. The right people were hard to find. The branding felt out of reach. The website felt like a mountain. Every step that should have taken a day took a week, and every week that passed felt like an opportunity quietly slipping away.

I got through it. But I never forgot how it felt, that specific, suffocating helplessness of having a vision and no clear road to get there. I asked myself a question that would change everything: "What if the next person did not have to go through this alone?"

That question became Startup Solution, a creative digital agency built from scar tissue and stubbornness, designed specifically so that founders, creators, and dreamers could launch their business with the design, the presence, and the confidence they deserve from day one. No more scattered tools. No more impossible price tags. No more starting from zero with nothing but a good idea and a prayer.

Every service at Startup Solution, every logo crafted, every ad edited, every website built, carries that original promise: that the next person who dares to start something should not have to struggle to be seen.
